title: The Officers Begin Losing Ground
description: Pages 3–7 focus on the officers begin losing ground, keeping the Dressrosa conflict and character roles clear for display.
scene: This part follows Luffy, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6 as the battles against Doflamingo's officers intensify as the allies begin turning Dressrosa's chaos into victories.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: With his friend standing in his way, what willuffy do.....?!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: There's no reason for you to pick this fight with me!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: That may be true, but I don't give a shit anymore!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: standin' there and I'll bring the pain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Plateau on which the Palace is located - second level

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #17
dialogue.text: This stubborn, chunk of rock just doesn't know when to quit!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #19
dialogue.text: Despite having learned what my powers are...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #20
dialogue.text: You still believe you can take me down. How naive.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #29
dialogue.text: Huh?! Why are we lined up at attention...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #32
dialogue.text: Are you someone from the Colos- Seum?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #33
dialogue.text: I am orlumbus! Had this been the sea, I'd have sent my grand yotta Maria fleet to aid you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #34
dialogue.text: We're a bit short on hands at the moment, so I'm going to have to leave that monster to you!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #35
dialogue.text: Yeah, I'll handle him... that bas- tard has been flailing about for too long already...

summary: The Officers Begin Losing Ground advances the Dressrosa story through Luffy, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, with unclear voices kept readable rather than forced into unsafe speaker assignments.
